ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (project manager, analyst, executive) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department ID of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the project */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Department ID of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "budget_ranges" (
  "budget_range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each budget range */,
  "budget_range_name" TEXT /* Name of the budget range */,
  "budget_range_values" TEXT /* Values of the budget range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("budget_range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_outcomes" (
  "goal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each goal range */,
  "budget_range_id" INTEGER /* Budget range ID */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the project outcome */,
  "number_successful" INTEGER /* Number of projects that were successful */,
  "number_failed" INTEGER /* Number of projects that failed */,
  "number_canceled" INTEGER /* Number of projects that were canceled */,
  "total_projects" INTEGER /* Total number of projects in the budget range */,
  "percentage_successful" REAL /* Percentage of projects that were successful */,
  "percentage_failed" REAL /* Percentage of projects that failed */,
  "percentage_canceled" REAL /* Percentage of projects that were canceled */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("goal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_metrics" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Project ID */,
  "success_rate" REAL /* Success rate of the project */,
  "failure_rate" REAL /* Failure rate of the project */,
  "cancellation_rate" REAL /* Cancellation rate of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date of report generation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)
column_names: Role of the user (project manager, analyst, executive)
Unique identifier for each budget range
Unique identifier for each goal range
Number of projects that were successful
Number of projects that were canceled
Total number of projects in the budget range
Percentage of projects that were successful
Percentage of projects that were canceled
